close_reason: Landed in e78f6d0fc. Added quoted-surface recognition for Fz.Compiler.define(source_root, __ENV__) and applied those service forms in source order through ScopeSession. Removed the delayed FunctionPlan/publish_functions path; literal function forms and protocol impl callbacks now publish FunctionSource through the same compiler-service helper, with telemetry at [fz, compiler2, compiler_service, define]. Added source-publication tests proving explicit service publication, namespace threading/self-binding, and no source-production authority for compiler-service-shaped calls inside runtime function bodies. Updated quoted-source, pipeline, and telemetry docs. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo check --all; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib compiler_service_define; cargo test --lib compiler2_; cargo test --lib; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; git diff --check.
description:
Goal: give source production a real compile-time `Fz.Compiler` service surface. Expanded forms call compiler services in source order, with `__ENV__` / caller scope carrying the active compiler authority; each service publishes quoted roots through the live `ScopeSession` before the source walk continues, and runtime/native execution never receives that authority. This replaces the current module-indexing shortcut that saves function-body AST while indexing: function AST is saved by `Fz.Compiler` only after expansion has reached the compiler-service call.

Signal:
- a compiler-service form such as `Fz.Compiler.define(root, __ENV__)` executes during source production and publishes the same FunctionSource, module, type, protocol, and impl facts as the equivalent literal source form
- function-body AST is no longer saved as a raw side effect of module indexing; the saved FunctionSource root comes from the fully expanded AST handed to `Fz.Compiler`
- by the time `Fz.Compiler.define` is called for a function, its AST root is macro/sugar-expanded enough for downstream function facts to consume without a second item-expansion path
- the service receives AST as an Fz `AnyValueRef` root on the active quoted source heap; `{heap, root}` remains the stable source key and no Rust-AST conversion boundary is introduced
- service evaluation threads an updated compile-time environment/session forward, so later forms in the same scope observe definitions introduced earlier by compiler services
- normal backend/native execution has no compiler-host environment; attempts to call compiler services fail without authority instead of mutating compiler state
- no `World` mutation occurs through unsafe interpreter aliases while `Types` is borrowed; compiler authority terminates at the source-production / `ScopeSession` boundary
- telemetry records compiler service calls with raw compiler ids, quoted-source keys, and the before/after source-session revision signal so tests can compare service publication to literal-source publication

Change:
- add the `Fz.Compiler` service surface and compile-time extern dispatch only for interpreter runs launched by source production
- model the compile-time host as an authority-bearing environment/session value, analogous to Elixir's `__ENV__`, not as a loose command buffer owned by macro execution
- expose a narrow `ScopeSession` entrypoint that publishes one fully expanded quoted source root in order and returns the updated session/environment needed by the next source form
- move the FunctionSource publication responsibility out of raw module indexing and into the `Fz.Compiler` service implementation; any existing helper that indexes function roots should become service-owned or disappear
- evaluate compiler-service forms during the source walk, immediately applying publication through the same source_publish machinery used for literal source
- add tests proving service-defined forms publish the same facts as literal forms, that raw module indexing no longer records unexpanded function bodies, that later source forms see earlier service publications, and that runtime roots cannot access compiler authority

Why:
- Elixir's `def` captures caller env, expands to a compiler call, and module-body evaluation executes that call against compiler-owned definition state; macro dispatch passes caller state as the hidden macro argument when needed
- the corresponding compiler2 shape is source-order service evaluation with `__ENV__`/scope threaded through the source session
- compiler-service calls are the point where expanded source becomes compiler facts; module indexing should not preempt that by saving raw function-body AST early
- compiler-service calls are source production, not macro executable readiness and not interpreter-owned mutation
- this keeps macro expansion, source publication, and fact production correct-by-construction through one source-tier authority boundary

References:
- `../elixir/lib/elixir/lib/kernel.ex`: `def` / `defmacro` call `define(..., __CALLER__)`, cache env, and expand to `:elixir_def.store_definition(...)`
- `../elixir/lib/elixir/src/elixir_def.erl`: `store_definition/3` recovers cached env and writes the compiler definition tables
- `../elixir/lib/elixir/src/elixir_dispatch.erl`: macro dispatch applies macro functions as `[Caller | Args]`
- `../elixir/lib/elixir/src/elixir_erl.erl`: macro lowering prepends hidden caller and materializes `__CALLER__` from it when used
title: Compiler2 macro host services: __ENV__-threaded Fz.Compiler publication
